cards.front: What is the purpose of the universal quantifier (∀)?
cards.back: The universal quantifier (∀) indicates that a statement is true for all elements of a set.

cards.front: What is the difference between ∀ and ∃?
cards.back: ∀ (for all) implies that a statement is true for every single element in a set, whereas ∃ (there exists) implies that at least one element in a set satisfies a statement.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the existential quantifier (∃)?
cards.back: The existential quantifier (∃) indicates that a statement is true for at least one element of a set.

cards.front: What is the order of operations for the quantifiers?
cards.back: The order of operations for the quantifiers is ∀ > ∃ (i.e., statements with ∀ are true for all elements, and statements with ∃ are true for at least one element).

cards.front: What is the meaning of the negated quantifier ¬?
cards.back: The negated quantifier ¬ (not) indicates that a statement is false for all elements of a set (¬∀), or that there does not exist at least one element that satisfies a statement (¬∃).
